skill.name: broken-yaml-skill
skill.description: Broken YAML fixture for packager failure testing.
skill.summary: Intentionally invalid YAML fixture used to verify packager error handling.
skill.author: yaojingang

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 3 potential issues (weak crypto, system recon), but all are false positives. The files contain only plain English text and intentionally malformed YAML with an unterminated quote on line 8 of interface.yaml. No executable code, no network calls, no system access patterns exist. This is a test fixture designed to trigger packager validation errors, not a functional skill.

content.user_title: Validate Packager Behavior with Broken YAML Fixtures
content.value_statement: Developers need reliable ways to test packager error handling when processing malformed input. This fixture provides an intentionally broken YAML file that triggers validation failures in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Provides a deliberately malformed YAML file for testing packager validation logic
Contains an unterminated string quote that triggers YAML parse errors
Serves as a known-bad input for regression testing skill packaging pipelines
Includes a minimal SKILL.md frontmatter to test partial parsing scenarios
Acts as a reference example for developers building robust skill loaders
limitations: Not a functional skill and cannot be loaded by Claude, Codex, or Claude Code
Contains intentional syntax errors and will fail YAML parsing
Has no executable code or useful runtime behavior for end users
Only useful as a testing artifact for skill packager developers

use_cases.title: Packager regression testing
use_cases.description: Use this fixture in automated tests to verify your skill packager correctly reports YAML parse errors instead of silently producing broken output.

use_cases.title: Error message validation
use_cases.description: Compare your packager error output against this fixture to ensure messages are clear and actionable when malformed YAML is encountered.

use_cases.title: CI pipeline integration testing
use_cases.description: Include this fixture in continuous integration test suites to catch regressions in YAML validation across skill releases.

prompt_templates.title: Basic fixture usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Load the broken-yaml-skill fixture and report the exact parse error produced.

prompt_templates.title: Packager error testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Run my skill packager against the broken-yaml-skill fixture and show the full error trace.

prompt_templates.title: Automated validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a test that asserts my packager returns a non-zero exit code and a YAML parse error message when processing the broken-yaml-skill fixture.

prompt_templates.title: CI integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a CI step that runs all skill fixtures including broken-yaml-skill to verify error handling remains consistent across releases.

output: Error: YAML parse failure in agents/interface.yaml line 8
Cause: Unterminated quoted string starting at line 8
Recommendation: Fix quote escaping in the source fixture before packaging.

output_examples.input: Run packager on broken-yaml-skill and summarize results.
output: Next step: Review fixture content for valid YAML syntax.

best_practices: Always run your packager against both valid and intentionally broken fixtures during development.
Document expected error messages so contributors can recognize regressions quickly.
Keep broken fixtures isolated in a test fixtures directory to avoid confusion with real skills.
anti_patterns: Do not ship intentionally broken fixtures in production skill packages.
Do not treat a YAML parse error as a silent warning without surfacing it to the user.
Do not modify the fixture to make it valid, as that defeats its testing purpose.

faq.question: What is this fixture used for?
faq.answer: It is a test fixture that provides intentionally malformed YAML so developers can verify their skill packager handles parse errors correctly.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code load this skill?
faq.answer: No. The YAML is intentionally invalid and will fail parsing, so none of these tools can load it as a functional skill.

faq.question: What error does the fixture trigger?
faq.answer: It triggers a YAML scanner exception caused by an unterminated quoted string in agents/interface.yaml line 8.

faq.question: Is it safe to include in my repository?
faq.answer: Yes. It contains only static text and no executable code, network requests, or system access patterns.

faq.question: How should I use it in tests?
faq.answer: Add it to your packager test suite and assert that processing it returns a clear error and a non-zero exit code.

faq.question: Should I fix the YAML to make it load?
faq.answer: No. Fixing it would remove the very property that makes it useful for error handling tests.
